2.1. Residual Stream

[TODO] this is not exhaustive enough because each head still goes to the residual stream. Each layer adds its results into the residual stream, which gives a decomposition of the output into terms1:

(2)

If we remove MLPs, then we get more terms. The recurrence relation for the number of terms in the residual stream after layer is , yielding terms:

(3)

A virtual weight is applied the output of an earlier layer when it travels through the residual stream to the input of a later layer:

The residual stream lives in the vector space . Empirically, we see that layers can interact by writing to and reading from the same overlaping subspaces. To visualize this, think about the and embeddings dictating what parts of the stream are read from or written to.

2.2. Tensor Notation

2.3. Zero-Layer Transformer

Approximates bigram plus absolute position bias.

2.4. Induction Heads

Two-layer transformers show an interesting online learning algorithm that mimick online bigrams via induction heads.

Thu May 21: Induction head kinda works like online (in-context) bigram estimator. Zero-layer transformer is a bigram but it learns from the data only.

Induction head let’s you autoregress better after seeing a sequence once.

[TODO] capstone project idea to investigate why induction heads pop up in the middle layers
